name: Serra de Busa and Vall d'Ora Loop
name_original: Sierra de Busa y Valle de Ora — Ruta circular por el Solsonès
description: This difficult 23.3-mile mountain biking route through Sierra de Busa and Valle de Ora offers challenging terrain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Navès offer a diverse landscape for riders, characterized by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and forested areas. The region features a network of routes that traverse through natural parks, past reservoirs, and along river valleys. Riders can expect varied elevations and technical sections, providing a comprehensive mountain biking experience. Best mountain bike trails around Navès The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra de Busa and Vall d'Ora Loop, a 23.3 miles (37.5 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete, winding through mountain ranges and scenic valleys. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Gorg de la Cascada – Saint Peter of Graudescales loop from Salt de Llinars, a difficult 22.2 miles (35.7 km) path. This route features a waterfall and passes by historical landmarks. Local mountain bikers also love the Solsona – Sant Ponç Reservoir loop from Navès, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail leading through the area around Sant Ponç Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Navès is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and reservoirs. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Navès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Navès?
A: Navès offers an extensive network of over 180 mountain bike trails. While many routes are challenging, catering to experienced riders, there are also options for moderate and even a few easier rides, ensuring a diverse experience for all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navès?
A: The ideal seasons for mountain biking in Navès are spring, summer, and autumn. During these months, the weather is generally pleasant, and trails are easily accessible, allowing riders to fully enjoy the region's diverse landscapes and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Navès?
A: While Navès is known for its challenging routes, there are approximately 8 easier mountain bike trails available. These routes provide a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride, allowing them to enjoy the scenic beauty without overly technical demands.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Navès?
A: Mountain biking in Navès features a diverse landscape, characterized by mountainous terrain, deep valleys, and forested areas. Riders will encounter varied elevations, from gentle slopes to steep peaks, along with technical sections, alpine meadows, rushing streams, and traditional villages. Some trails may include challenging elements like berms, rock gardens, and switchbacks.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails in Navès that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, some routes in Navès pass by beautiful natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Cascada – Saint Peter of Graudescales loop from Salt de Llinars&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult trail that specifically features a waterfall, offering a refreshing stop along the way.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Navès?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Navès are designed as loops, allowing riders to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949690163/serra-de-busa-and-vall-d-ora-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Busa and Vall d'Ora Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona – Sant Ponç Reservoir loop from Navès&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you around the scenic Sant Ponç Reservoir.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails in Navès?
A: The trails around Navès offer access to several notable landmarks and viewpoints. You can explore mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Busa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Jou&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4863016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queralt Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning panoramic views. Some routes also pass by historical sites and picturesque villages.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Navès?
A: The mountain bike trails in Navès are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders frequently praise the region's diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning natural landscapes, and the variety of routes that cater to different skill levels, from technical single trails to scenic loops around reservoirs.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: Yes, Navès is well-suited for advanced riders, with approximately 90 difficult trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aigua d'Ora loop from Llinars&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31534229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi de les tres branques – View of the Serra de Queralt loop from Salt de Llinars&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, providing a demanding and rewarding experience for those seeking an adrenaline rush.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the area around Sant Ponç Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38184386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona – Sant Ponç Reservoir loop from Navès&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that specifically leads through the scenic area around Sant Ponç Reservoir. It's a great option for enjoying the water views and surrounding natural beauty.
tips: Declared a cultural asset of national interest. It is located in La Vall d'Ora, in the middle of a beautiful landscape. The current church dates from the 12th century and is considered Romanesque in style.
At the foot of the walls of the Busa, at more than 1,000 meters above sea level, we find this old stone building converted into an ideal hostel for groups and youth camps.
Church of the XI century and Romanesque style. Located in the municipality of Navés, in the region of Solsonés, Lérida.
Magnificent views of the Valle de Ora and the Llosa del Cavall marsh from the Sierra de Busa

The natural prison of Busa is located in the Capotell. A plane surrounded by cliffs where it could only be accessed by a bridge that connected with this type of natural island that was placed and removed during the French War to enter prisoners.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Sierra de Busa and Valle de Ora mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It's approximately 37.5 km long with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, featuring demanding ascents with gradients up to 21%. A good level of physical fitness and mountain biking experience is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, primarily consisting of gravel paths, with some asphalt sections and singletrack segments. You'll encounter wide dirt paths, but also rocky elements, especially on descents. Be prepared for significant climbs and varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular panoramic views and passes several notable sites. You'll encounter the historic Saint Peter of Graudescales, the Rectoria de La Selva, and the Church of Sant Martí de les Sorgues. Don't miss the dramatic The Capolatell (Busa Prison), a natural monument, and the View of the Llosa del Cavall Reservoir from Mirador de Capolat.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails, including the TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus, Vall d'Ora-Pla de Busa-Capolatell, Ruta del Caracremada, and Sender Històric: Catalunya, among others.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in the Solsonès region, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather or snow at higher elevations, making some sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Busa and Valle de Ora route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in this region, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the mixed terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking this route within the Serra de Busa - els Bastets - Lord area. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, you can typically start and park in a suitable location near the beginning of the loop. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, look for designated parking in nearby villages or trailheads. The route starts near the 11th-century Romanesque church of Correa, which might offer parking options.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or eat along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers opportunities to experience local culture and gastronomy. Masia el Pujol is mentioned as a place where you can refuel and enjoy traditional food and drink, providing a welcome break during your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and steep gradients, this route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires a good level of fitness and mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking natural landscapes, including the dramatic rock formations of the Pla de Busa, which resembles an island. The route offers spectacular panoramic views of the Solsonès comarca, with its varied flora and fauna, and glimpses of historical sites.
